title: lilium pardalinum subsp. wigginsii
intro: Lilium pardalinum subsp. wigginsii, also known as Wiggins' lily or Wiggins' mariposa lily, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the mariposa lily, a perennial bulbous plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Lilium pardalinium subsp. wigginsii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
